title: Model Performance Degradation Detection (Art. 12)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Art.15.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that EU AI Act Art.15 (Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.21.4: Model Performance Degradation Detection (Art. 12).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.21.4' running on index=ai_metrics sourcetype=ai:model:kpi — model_id, metric_name, metric_value, window, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk ITSI (1841)
spl:
index=ai_metrics sourcetype="ai:model:kpi" metric_name="accuracy" earliest=-7d
| timechart span=1h perc95(metric_value) as p95_acc by model_id
| untable _time model_id p95_acc
| eventstats median(p95_acc) as baseline by model_id
| eval delta=p95_acc-baseline
| where delta < -0.02
| table _time, model_id, p95_acc, baseline, delta
description: Detects sudden drops in accuracy/auroc vs baseline to support operational logging and risk management for high-risk AI performance stability.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ai_metrics and sourcetypes ai:model:kpi, ai:model:kpi`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Ingest batch evaluation KPIs hourly; (2) tune -0.02 threshold per model risk file; (3) create ITSI KPI thresholding; (4) document rollback criteria in QMS. Indexes required: `index=ai_metrics`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ai:model:kpi`, `sourcetype=ai:model:kpi``.
visualization: Time chart (p95_acc vs baseline), Table (negative deltas), Single value (models in alert)
knownFalsePositives: High-risk system events during model retraining, scheduled validation runs, or feature rollouts.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk ITSI (1841).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ai_metrics` `sourcetype=ai:model:kpi` — model_id, metric_name, metric_value, window.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest batch evaluation KPIs hourly; (2) tune -0.02 threshold per model risk file; (3) create ITSI KPI thresholding; (4) document rollback criteria in QMS.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ai_metrics sourcetype="ai:model:kpi" metric_name="accuracy" earliest=-7d
| timechart span=1h perc95(metric_value) as p95_acc by model_id
| untable _time model_id p95_acc
| eventstats median(p95_acc) as baseline by model_id
| eval delta=p95_acc-baseline
| where delta < -0.02
| table _time, model_id, p95_acc, baseline, delta
```

Understanding this SPL

**Model Performance Degradation Detection (Art. 12) (EU AI Act)** — Detects sudden drops in accuracy/auroc vs baseline to support operational logging and risk management for high-risk AI performance stability.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ai_metrics` `sourcetype=ai:model:kpi` — model_id, metric_name, metric_value, window. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk ITSI (1841). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ai_metrics; **sourcetype**: ai:model:kpi. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ai_metrics, sourcetype="ai:model:kpi", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets with a separate series **by model_id** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by model_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **delta** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where delta < -0.02` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: _time, baseline, delta, model_id, p95_acc.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the AI model registry, MLOps pipeline, and the ServiceNow GRC console for the same validation window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart (p95_acc vs baseline), Table (negative deltas), Single value (models in alert)
grandmaExplanation: We look for sudden drops in accuracy/auroc vs baseline to support operational logging and risk management for high-risk AI performance stability.
